member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDockCommand.CanExecute(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the command may execute. The command may be executed in the following cases: - The currently active form is the one where the associated RadDock resides. - The currently active form is a FloatingWindow instance, owned by the associated RadDock. - The currently active form is an AutoHidePopup instance, owned by the associated RadDock.
member.param: The additional parameter provided. Should be a RadDock instance.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.HostWindow.ActivateAsMdiChild
member.summary: Activates the hosted form as an MDI child window.
member.remarks: This method uses reflection to access the non-public ActivateMdiChild method of the Form class, allowing the hosted MDI child form to become the active MDI child in the parent MDI container. This is used for proper MDI window management when integrating with the standard Windows Forms MDI system.

member.name: T:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock
member.summary:
Represents the major control in the Telerik.WinForms.UI.Docking assembly. Root component for the entire docking framework. Provides highly extensible and completely transparent transaction-based API for managing a set of tool and document windows registered within the docking framework. Mimics the user experience found in Microsoft Visual Studio - dockable panes, tabbed documents, document navigation plus much more. A typical scenario of utilizing a docking framework is as follows:
Create a new RadDock instance.
Create the desired ToolWindow and/or DocumentWindow instances.
Register the newly created dock windows with the RadDock using the DockWindow or the AddDocument methods respectively.
Add the new RadDock instance to a Form.
Display the Form.
Although a rare scenario, sometimes the need of nested RadDock instances may emerge. This is fully supported by the RadDock component and unlimited levels of nesting are allowed.

member.summary: Initializes a new RadDock instance.

member.summary: Releases the unmanaged resources used by the and optionally releases the managed resources.
member.param: true to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources.
member.remarks: This method cleans up all resources associated with the docking framework, including command managers, document managers, MDI controllers, and floating windows.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.AutoHideAnimationDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the duration of the auto hide window animation. The default value is 200 milliseconds.
member.value: The duration of the auto hide window animation. The default value is 200 milliseconds.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.AutoHidePopup
member.summary: Gets the popup control used for displaying auto-hide windows.
member.returns: An instance that manages the display of auto-hidden windows.
member.remarks: The auto-hide popup is displayed when the user hovers over an auto-hide tab, showing the content of the associated docking window temporarily.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnParentChanged(System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Raises the event.
member.param: An that contains the event data.
member.remarks: This method updates the internal parent form reference and reconnects event handlers when the parent of the RadDock control changes. It's essential for proper operation of floating windows and auto-hide functionality.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.CloseAutoHidePopup
member.summary: Closes the popup of the currently opened auto-hide window.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.autoHideTimer_Tick(System.Object,System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Handles the Tick event of the auto-hide timer.
member.param: The source of the event.
The instance containing the event data.
member.remarks: This method manages the display and hiding of auto-hide windows based on mouse position and user interaction. It determines whether to show or hide the auto-hide popup depending on which tab item (if any) is under the mouse cursor.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.HitTestTabStipItem
member.summary: Determines which tab strip item is under the current mouse position.
member.returns: The at the current mouse position, or null if none.
member.remarks: This method is used by the auto-hide system to detect when the mouse is over an auto-hide tab, which triggers the display of the associated window.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.GetAutoHideTab(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Gets the tab strip item associated with an auto-hidden dock window.
member.param: The dock window for which to find the auto-hide tab.
member.returns: The representing the auto-hide tab for the specified window, or null if the window is not in auto-hide mode or doesn't have an associated tab.
member.remarks: Auto-hide tabs are displayed along the edges of the docking container and allow users to temporarily show windows that are in auto-hide mode.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.GetAutoHideTabStrip(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.AutoHidePosition)
member.summary: Gets the auto hide tab strip.
member.param: The edge position of the auto-hide strip to retrieve.
member.returns: A representing the tab strip at the specified position.
member.remarks: The tab strip contains the tabs that represent auto-hidden windows. When the user hovers over one of these tabs, the corresponding window is temporarily displayed.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.BeginUpdate
member.summary: Begins an update operation. Internally used by the transaction mechanism.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.EndUpdate
member.summary: Ends a BeginUpdate block.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.EndUpdate(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Ends a BeginUpdate block and optionally performs layout update.
member.param: Specifies whether to perform layout operations after ending the update block. When true, layout operations are performed. When false, layout remains suspended.
member.remarks: Use this method to end an update operation that was started with BeginUpdate. The update parameter allows control over whether layout is immediately performed or remains suspended.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.PerformUpdate
member.summary: Performs the core update logic after an EndUpdate call.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.CleanUp
member.summary: Performs a clean-up pass which removes all unnecessary internally created split panels and/or collapses or disposes them.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.BeginTransactionBlock
member.summary: Opens a transaction (batch) operation. This is used by the framework to indicate some lengthy operations, during which no updates should be performed.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.BeginTransactionBlock(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Opens a transaction (batch) operation. This is used by the framework to indicate some lengthy operations, during which no updates should be performed.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.EnqueueTransaction(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDockTransaction)
member.summary: Adds a transaction operation to the current transaction block.
member.param: The transaction to add to the current block. Must not be null.
member.remarks: This method adds a dock operation to the current transaction. Transactions are executed as a batch when the transaction block is closed. A transaction block must be opened with BeginTransactionBlock before this method can be called.
member.exception: Thrown when the transaction parameter is null.
Thrown when there is no active transaction block.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FlushTransactions
member.summary: Commits all pending transactions without exitting the transaction block.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.IsInTransactionBlock
member.summary: Determines whether the RadDock is currently in a transaction (internal operation).

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.EndTransactionBlock
member.summary: Ends the current transaction block and commits all pending transactions.
member.remarks: This method decrements the transaction block counter. When the counter reaches zero, all pending transactions are executed, and normal layout operations are resumed. Each call to EndTransactionBlock should correspond to a previous call to BeginTransactionBlock.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.RegisterTransaction(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDockTransaction,System.Boolean)
member.summary: An internal helper method that will either enqueue the transaction if a block is currently running or will open block, commit the transaction and close that block.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FloatWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Makes the specified window floating (detached in its own window).
member.param: The DockWindow instance to make floating.
member.remarks: This method detaches the window from its current position in the docking layout and displays it in a separate floating window. It uses default bounds for the new floating window. To specify the bounds of the floating window, use the overload that accepts a Rectangle parameter.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FloatWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow,System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Makes the specified window floating (detached in its own window), using the provided bounds.
member.param: The DockWindow instance to make floating.
The desired floating bounds. Pass Rectangle.Empty to set the default bounds based on the window's preferred size.
member.remarks: This method detaches the window from its current position in the docking layout and displays it in a separate floating window with the specified bounds. The DockType of the container in the floating window is automatically determined based on whether the window is a DocumentWindow or not.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FloatWindows(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow},System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Makes the specified collection of windows floating (detached in a single window), using the provided bounds.
member.param: The collection of DockWindow instances to make floating together.
The desired floating bounds. Pass Rectangle.Empty to set the default bounds based on the windows' preferred sizes.
member.remarks: This method detaches all the specified windows from their current positions in the docking layout and displays them together in a single floating window with the specified bounds. All windows will be arranged in a tab strip within the floating window. This overload uses DockType.ToolWindow as the container type. For document windows, use the overload that accepts a DockType parameter.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FloatWindows(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow},System.Drawing.Rectangle,Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockType)
member.summary: Makes the specified windows floating (detached in a single window), using the provided bounds and container type.
member.param: The collection of DockWindow instances to make floating together.
The desired floating bounds. Pass Rectangle.Empty to set the default bounds based on the windows' preferred sizes.
The type of the container inside the floating form - DockType.ToolWindow or DockType.Document.
member.remarks: This method detaches all the specified windows from their current positions in the docking layout and displays them together in a single floating window with the specified bounds. All windows will be arranged in a tab strip of the specified type within the floating window. The container type determines the appearance and behavior of the tab strip in the floating window.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FloatToolTabStrip(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolTabStrip,System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Makes the specified tool tab strip floating (detached in its own window).
member.param: The ToolTabStrip to make floating.
The desired floating bounds. Pass Rectangle.Empty to set the default bounds based on the strip's preferred size.
member.remarks: This method detaches the entire tool tab strip, including all contained windows, from its current position in the docking layout and displays it in a separate floating window with the specified bounds. The tab structure is preserved in the floating window - all windows that were in the tab strip remain grouped together.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.AutoHideWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Makes the specified DockWindow auto-hidden along an edge of the docking layout.
member.param: The DockWindow instance to auto-hide.
member.remarks:
This method changes the dock state of the window to auto-hide, making it collapse to a tab along an edge of the docking layout. When auto-hidden, the window will slide out when the user hovers over or clicks its tab.
If the window is part of a tab strip, the entire tab strip will be auto-hidden. The auto-hide position (Left, Right, Top, Bottom) is determined based on the window's current position in the layout.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.AutoHideWindows(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow},Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.AutoHidePosition)
member.summary: Makes the specified collection of DockWindow instances auto-hidden at a specific edge.
member.param: The collection of DockWindow instances to auto-hide.
The edge position (Left, Right, Top, Bottom) at which the windows should be auto-hidden.
member.remarks:
This method changes the dock state of all specified windows to auto-hide, making them collapse to tabs along the specified edge of the docking layout. When auto-hidden, the windows will slide out when the user hovers over or clicks their tabs.
All windows specified will be grouped together in the auto-hide area, with each window represented by a tab.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.CloseWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Removes or hides the specified window based on its CloseAction property.
member.param: The DockWindow instance to close.
member.remarks:
This method closes the specified window according to its CloseAction property: - CloseAction.Close: Removes the window from the layout without disposing it - CloseAction.CloseAndDispose: Removes the window and disposes it - CloseAction.Hide: Hides the window but keeps it registered with the docking manager
If the window is already hidden (DockState.Hidden) and its CloseAction is Hide, this method does nothing.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.CloseWindows(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow})
member.summary: Removes or hides the specified collection of windows based on their CloseAction properties.
member.param: The collection of DockWindow instances to close.
member.remarks:
This method closes each window in the collection according to its CloseAction property: - CloseAction.Close: Removes the window from the layout without disposing it - CloseAction.CloseAndDispose: Removes the window and disposes it - CloseAction.Hide: Hides the window but keeps it registered with the docking manager
The operation is performed as a single transaction, ensuring consistent behavior and proper events. Before closing each window, a DockWindowClosing event is raised, which can be handled to cancel the operation for specific windows.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.DisplayWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Displays the specified window if it was previously hidden, restoring it to its previous dock state.
member.param: The DockWindow instance to display.
member.remarks:
This method restores a hidden window to its previous dock state. If the window was not previously hidden or is not registered with this RadDock instance, this method has no effect.
The window will be displayed in the same location it was in before being hidden, whether that was docked, floating, auto-hidden, or as a tabbed document.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.DisplayWindows(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow})
member.summary: Displays the provided collection of dock windows if they were previously hidden and are registered with this RadDock instance.
member.param: The collection of DockWindow instances to display.
member.remarks:
This method restores hidden windows to their previous dock states. Only windows that meet all of the following criteria are affected: - The window is registered with this RadDock instance - The window is currently hidden (DockState.Hidden) - The window has a valid previous dock state to restore to
Each window will be displayed in the same location it was in before being hidden, whether that was docked, floating, auto-hidden, or as a tabbed document.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SetWindowState(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow,Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockState)
member.summary: Applies the desired DockState to the specified DockWindow, either by restoring a previously saved state or performing the default action.
member.param: The DockWindow instance to change state for.
The target dock state to apply (Docked, TabbedDocument, AutoHide, Floating, Hidden).
member.remarks:
This method changes the dock state of the window to the specified state. If the window is already in the requested state, this method has no effect.
If a previously saved layout state exists for this window in the requested dock state (through the RedockService), that state will be restored, preserving the window's previous position and size in that state. Otherwise, the default action will be performed: - DockState.Docked: Window will be docked to the left side - DockState.TabbedDocument: Window will be added as a document - DockState.AutoHide: Window will be auto-hidden on the left side - DockState.Floating: Window will be floated - DockState.Hidden: Window will be hidden

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.CommandManager
member.summary: Gets the CommandManager registered with this RadDock instance.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnHandleDestroyed(System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Called when the control's handle is destroyed. Disables the command manager to prevent stray commands.
member.param: An EventArgs that contains the event data.
member.remarks: This override ensures that the command manager is disabled when the control's handle is destroyed, preventing any command processing after the handle is gone but the control is still alive.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnEnabledChanged(System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Called when the control's Enabled property changes. Synchronizes the command manager's enabled state.
member.param: An EventArgs that contains the event data.
member.remarks: This override ensures that the command manager's enabled state is synchronized with the control's Enabled property, preventing command processing when the control is disabled.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.MainDocumentContainerVisible
member.summary: Determines whether the main document container is visible.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.RegisterService(System.Int32,Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDockService)
member.summary: Registers the specified service with this RadDock instance.
member.param: The key that identifies the service, typically a value from ServiceConstants.
The RadDockService instance to register.
member.remarks: This method registers a service that provides additional functionality to the RadDock control. If a service with the same key already exists, it will be detached and replaced with the new service. Services are used to extend RadDock functionality without modifying its core code. Common services include RedockService for state persistence and CommandService for command routing.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.GetService``1
member.summary: Retrieves the currently registered service by the specified type.
member.typeparam: The type of service to retrieve. Must be a subclass of RadDockService.
member.returns: The registered service of type T, or null if no such service is registered.
member.remarks: This method searches all registered services for one that is of the specified type T. It returns the first matching service found, or null if no service of type T is registered. This method is useful when you know what kind of service you need but don't know its key.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.GetService``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the currently registered service by the provided key.
member.typeparam: The type of service to retrieve. Must be a subclass of RadDockService.
member.param: The key that identifies the service, typically a value from ServiceConstants.
member.returns: The registered service of type T associated with the specified key, or null if no such service is registered.
member.remarks: This method retrieves a service using its key. All predefined service keys can be found in the ServiceConstants class. It returns the service cast to type T, or null if either no service is registered with the key or the registered service is not of type T.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.IsDragging

member.summary: Raises the DockWindowSerializing event.
member.returns: [true] if canceled, [false] otherwise.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnLoadedFromXml
member.summary: Raises the LoadedFromXml event.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnSavedToXml
member.summary: Raises the SavedToXml event.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.LoadFromXml(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Loads the docking layout configuration from an XML file.
member.param: The path to the XML file containing the saved layout.
When set to true, removes all existing dock windows using the action before loading the new layout.
member.remarks:
This method automatically detects whether the XML file was created with the legacy DockingManager or with the current RadDock, and adapts its loading strategy accordingly. This enables backward compatibility with layouts saved by older versions.
The XML file contains information about window positions, sizes, dock states, and relationships between windows. Windows that need to be restored must already be registered with this RadDock instance or they will be ignored during loading.
To save the current layout to an XML file, use the SaveToXml method and any of its overloads.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.LoadFromXml(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Loads the docking layout configuration from an XML stream.
member.param: The stream containing the XML layout data. The stream must be readable and its position will be reset to the beginning.
When set to true, removes all existing dock windows using the action before loading the new layout.
member.remarks:
This method automatically detects whether the XML in the stream was created with the legacy DockingManager or with the current RadDock, and adapts its loading strategy accordingly. This enables backward compatibility with layouts saved by older versions.
The XML stream should contain information about window positions, sizes, dock states, and relationships between windows. Windows that need to be restored must already be registered with this RadDock instance or they will be ignored during loading.
This overload is useful when the layout is stored in memory or in a database rather than in a file. To save the current layout to a stream, use the method.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.LoadFromXml(System.IO.TextReader,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Loads the docking layout configuration from a TextReader. Note that this is a new method and does not support loading xml that was written using DockingManager.
member.param: The text reader.
if set to true removes the existing dock windows using the action.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.LoadFromXmlCore(System.Xml.XmlReader,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Overrwrite this method to change the loading of docking layout configuration.
member.param: The XmlReader instance that will be used to load the layoiut configuration.
if set to true the layout configuration is in the old DockingManager format.
if set to true removes the existing dock windows using the action.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.CreateXmlWriterSettings
member.summary: Creates the core XmlWritterSettings to be used by the serialization manager.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SaveToXml(System.String)
member.summary: Saves the current docking layout configuration in XML format to the specified file.
member.param: The path to the file where the layout should be saved. If the file exists, it will be overwritten.
member.remarks:
This method serializes the current state of all dock windows, including their positions, sizes, dock states, and relationships. The resulting XML file can later be used to restore the layout using the method.
The serialized data includes: - Window positions and sizes - Dock states (docked, floating, auto-hidden, etc.) - Tab groups and arrangements - Window relationships and hierarchy
Note that only the structural information is saved, not the actual content of the windows. When restoring a layout, the windows must already be registered with the RadDock instance.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SaveToXml(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Saves the current docking layout configuration in XML format to the specified stream.
member.param: The stream where the layout should be written. The stream must be writable.
member.remarks:
This method serializes the current state of all dock windows to the provided stream. This overload is useful when you need to save the layout to memory, a database, or another destination other than a file.
The serialized data includes: - Window positions and sizes - Dock states (docked, floating, auto-hidden, etc.) - Tab groups and arrangements - Window relationships and hierarchy
To restore a layout from a stream, use the method.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SaveToXml(System.IO.TextWriter)
member.summary: Saves the current docking layout configuration in XML format using the specified TextWriter.
member.param: The TextWriter instance to which the XML should be written.
member.remarks:
This method serializes the current state of all dock windows using the provided TextWriter. This overload is useful when you need fine-grained control over the text output process, such as when writing to a custom text destination or applying additional text processing.
The serialized data includes the same information as the other SaveToXml methods: - Window positions and sizes - Dock states (docked, floating, auto-hidden, etc.) - Tab groups and arrangements - Window relationships and hierarchy
The XML is formatted with proper indentation for readability.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.GetDefaultXmlSerializationInfo
member.summary: Gets the default serialization information for RadDock used by Save/Load layout methods to persist docking settings to/from XML.
member.remarks:
You can use the serialization information to customize which properties of RadDock and related objects are included in or excluded from XML serialization. This allows you to control exactly what aspects of the docking layout are persisted.
To customize serialization, you can: 1. Override this method in a derived class to return modified serialization information 2. Modify the XmlSerializationInfo property directly
Common customizations include: - Excluding specific properties that shouldn't be persisted - Adding custom properties that should be persisted - Changing serialization behavior for certain object types
member.returns: A ComponentXmlSerializationInfo object containing the default serialization settings for RadDock.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.XmlSerializationInfo
member.summary: Gets the serialization info for RadDock used by Save/Load loyout methods to persist grid settings to/from XML. By default, or when set to null the ComponentXmlSerializationInfo provided by GetDefaultXmlSerializationInfo() will be used.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SaveToXmlCore(System.Xml.XmlWriter)
member.summary: Performs the core XML serialization logic for saving the docking layout.
member.param: The XmlWriter to which the XML should be written.
member.remarks:
This method contains the actual implementation of the XML serialization process. It sorts floating windows by Z-order, creates a serializer with the appropriate settings, and writes the RadDock object structure to XML.
This method can be overridden in derived classes to customize the serialization process while still using the basic SaveToXml methods.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.GuidToNameMappings
member.summary: Gets a collection of mappings between GUIDs and window names used during serialization and deserialization.
member.remarks:
This collection maintains the associations between window GUIDs and their names, which is critical for correctly restoring windows during deserialization.
The GUID-to-name mappings ensure that windows can be identified consistently across serialization operations, even if window names change. This is particularly important when loading layouts in applications where window instances may be recreated with different names but should still be recognized as the same logical windows.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SerializableAutoHideContainer
member.summary: Gets an instance of DockAutoHideSerializationContainer that contains information about auto-hidden windows which have been set up through designer or xml file. This nethod is no intended for use directly from your code.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SerializableFloatingWindows
member.summary: Gets an instance of FloatingWindowList that contains information about floating windows which have been set up through designer or xml file. This nethod is no intended for use directly from your code.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.LoadDeserializedFloatingAndAutoHideWindows
member.summary: Loads and reconstructs floating and auto-hidden windows from serialized data.
member.remarks:
This method is intended for use within RadDock and Advanced Layout Designer to restore floating and auto-hidden windows after loading RadDock layout from the designer or from XML.
It reconstructs floating windows from their serialized representation, positions them correctly, and maintains their Z-order. After restoration, the temporary serializable objects are disposed to free up resources. The method also ensures redock states are properly initialized for state persistence.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.UpdateFloatingWindowsZOrderInfo
member.summary: Updates the Z-order information for all floating windows associated with this RadDock instance.
member.remarks: This method captures the current Z-order of floating windows by querying the Windows API, and then updates each FloatingWindow's ZIndex property accordingly. This ensures that when the layout is saved and later restored, the windows maintain their front-to-back ordering relationships.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ThemeClassName
member.summary: Gets or sets the theme class name used for applying visual styles to this control.
member.remarks:
The theme class name is used by the theming engine to identify which visual styles should be applied to this control. For RadDock, this property always returns the full name of the RadDock class, ensuring consistent styling across all instances.
This property is overridden to provide a fixed value and should not be modified directly.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.BackColor
member.summary: RadDock consists of multiple visual elements and separate settings are provided to customize their appearance. Current BackColor property might be ignored.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ForeColor
member.summary: RadDock consists of multiple visual elements and separate settings are provided to customize their appearance. Current ForeColor property might be ignored.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnDockableDeserialized(Telerik.WinControls.Interfaces.DockableDeserializedEventArgs)
member.summary: Raises the DockableDeserialized event for compatibility with the obsolete DockingManager XML format.
member.param: Event arguments containing information about the deserialized dockable object.
member.remarks: This method is called internally during the deserialization process of legacy DockingManager XML formats. It notifies listeners about each dockable object as it's deserialized, allowing for special handling of legacy components. This is primarily used for backward compatibility with older layouts.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ActiveWindow
member.summary: Gets or sets the DockWindow instance, which is currently active (meaning it contains the Keyboard focus).

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SetDefaultActiveWindow
member.summary: Sets the first available DockWindow as the currently active window.
member.remarks: This method searches for an available dock window to activate when no window is currently active. It first searches for windows directly residing on the RadDock control, and if none are found, it searches through all floating windows. This ensures that there is always an active window when possible, providing a better user experience and ensuring that keyboard focus is properly handled.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.SetDefaultActiveWindow(System.Windows.Forms.Control)
member.summary: Sets the first available DockWindow within the specified parent control as the currently active window.
member.param: The parent control to search for dock windows within.
member.remarks: This method searches through the child controls of the specified parent to find a valid DockTabStrip with dock windows. It then sets the first valid window it finds as the active window. This method is called by the parameterless SetDefaultActiveWindow method to search in both the main RadDock control and floating windows.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ActivateWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Activates the specified DockWindow and gives it the keyboard focus.
member.param: The DockWindow to activate. If null, no window will be activated.
member.returns: True if the window was successfully activated and has focus; otherwise, false.
member.remarks:
This method activates the specified window by: - Making it the active window in the RadDock control - Ensuring it's visible (sliding out if auto-hidden) - Giving it keyboard focus - Updating its visual state to appear active - Raising appropriate ActiveWindowChanging and ActiveWindowChanged events
This method is equivalent to calling ActivateWindow(window, false).

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ActivateWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Activates the specified window and gives it the keyboard focus.
member.param: True to force ActiveWindowChanging and ActiveWindowChanged notifications, false otherwise. Sometimes a transaction may change the current active window several times and the method needs to know when to raise the notifications.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.UpdateActiveWindow(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Updates the active window and performs all necessary actions to properly handle the window activation.
member.param: The window to activate.
Whether to force notification events even if the active window hasn't changed.
member.remarks:
This method performs the core logic for window activation, including: - Raising the ActiveWindowChanging event and checking for cancellation - Handling tab selection changes and their events - Managing auto-hide windows that need to be displayed - Updating visual states of both the previous and new active windows - Handling MDI child activation if auto-detect is enabled - Raising the ActiveWindowChanged event
The method is called internally by ActivateWindow and should not be called directly.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.UpdateActiveControl(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.DockWindow)
member.summary: Updates the active control within the specified window by traversing the control hierarchy.
member.param: The window whose active control should be updated.
member.remarks:
This method traverses the control hierarchy of the specified window to find the deepest active container control. It then ensures that this control has proper focus, which provides a better user experience by maintaining focus on the actual control the user was interacting with before a window change.
The method handles special cases for HostWindow controls, which wrap user-provided controls.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.FloatingWindows
member.summary: Gets a list of the floating windows. Can contain duplicates, empty or hidden toolwindows which are used internally as re-dock targets.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ActiveFloatingWindows
member.summary: Gets a read-only collection of all active floating windows managed by this RadDock instance.
member.remarks: This property provides access to all floating windows that contain valid dock windows. Floating windows that are disposed or don't contain valid dock windows are filtered out. This collection is useful for operations that need to work with only the currently active floating windows.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.ContainsDockWindows(Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.FloatingWindow)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified floating window contains any valid dock windows.
member.param: The floating window to check.
member.returns: True if the floating window is valid and contains at least one non-disposed DockWindow; otherwise, false.
member.remarks:
This method checks if the specified floating window is valid (not null, not disposed) and contains at least one valid DockWindow instance. It's used by the ActiveFloatingWindows property to filter out floating windows that don't contain any usable dock windows.
This filtering is important because floating windows may be in various states: - They may be empty placeholders - They may be in the process of being disposed - They may contain only disposed dock windows
By filtering these out, we ensure that operations on ActiveFloatingWindows only affect windows with valid content.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.OnBackgroundImageChanged(System.EventArgs)
member.summary: Called when the BackgroundImage property changes.
member.param: An EventArgs that contains the event data.
member.remarks: This override updates the visibility settings of various elements based on whether a background image is present.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.RadDock.UpdateBackgroundSettings
member.summary: Updates the visibility settings of split panel elements based on whether a background image is set.
member.remarks: When a background image is set, certain panel elements are hidden to allow the background image to show through. When no background image is set, the elements' visibility is reset to their default values.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolWindow.AutoHide
member.summary: Changes the window state to auto-hide mode.
member.remarks: When in auto-hide mode, the window collapses to a tab on the edge of its dock container, and expands when the mouse hovers over the tab. If the window is already in auto-hide mode or is not managed by a DockManager, this method has no effect.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolWindow.Float(System.Drawing.Point,System.Drawing.Size)
member.summary: Changes the window to floating mode with the specified location and size.
member.param: The screen coordinates where the floating window should appear.
The size of the floating window.
member.remarks: This method creates a floating window containing this tool window at the specified location and size. If the window is not managed by a DockManager, this method has no effect.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolWindow.Float(System.Drawing.Rectangle)
member.summary: Changes the window to floating mode with the specified bounds.
member.param: The screen coordinates and size of the floating window.
member.remarks: This method creates a floating window containing this tool window with the specified bounds. If the window is not managed by a DockManager, this method has no effect.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolWindow.CloseAction
member.summary: Gets or sets the action that occurs when this tool window is closed.
member.returns: A value indicating the behavior when the window is closed. The default value is .
member.remarks: Tool windows typically hide rather than dispose when closed, allowing them to be reopened later. This differs from document windows which often dispose their resources on close.

member.name: P:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolWindow.Caption
member.summary: Gets or sets the caption text displayed in the tool window's header area.
member.remarks: The caption is displayed in the parent ToolTabStrip when this window is active. Setting this property will update the parent strip's caption text and raise a property changed notification.

member.summary: Updates the window's visual representation when the Text property has changed.
member.remarks: This method synchronizes the Text property with the parent controls, including updating the caption of floating windows and triggering caption updates.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.ToolWindow.UpdateOnCaptionChanged
member.summary: Updates the parent ToolTabStrip's caption when this window's caption has changed.
member.remarks: This method is called whenever the Caption property changes or when the Text property changes. It ensures that the parent strip displays the correct caption text when this window is active.

member.summary: Default constructor.

member.summary: Pauses a currently running operation.

member.summary: Resumes previously paused operation.

member.name: M:Telerik.WinControls.UI.Docking.StateService.CanStart(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the service may be started. Validation is as follows: 1. Check whether Enabled is true. 2. Check the context through IsContextValid method. An exception is thrown if context is invalid. 3. Checks the current state - it should be Initial or Stopped.

member.summary: Notifies that the service has been successfully started. Allows inheritors to perform some additional logic upon start.

member.summary: Notifies that a start request has occured. Cancelable.

member.summary: Notifies that a running operation has stopped. Allows inheritors to perform some additional logic upon stop.

member.summary: Notifies that a stop request has occured. Cancelable.

member.summary: Stops the service. Performs the core logic.

member.summary: Aborts the current operation without applying any changes.
